quiz_questions.question_text: The four main topics on the AP Statistics exam are:

options.text: Exploring Data, Sampling & Experiments, Probability, Statistical Inference

quiz_questions.explanation: The AP framework covers these four broad content areas.

quiz_questions.question_text: To earn full credit on a free-response question:

options.text: State hypotheses, check conditions, show calculations, and conclude in context

quiz_questions.explanation: Each component earns points on the rubric.

quiz_questions.question_text: The difference between an experiment and an observational study is:

options.text: Experiments impose treatments; observational studies only observe without intervention

quiz_questions.explanation: Random assignment of treatments is what makes a study an experiment.

quiz_questions.question_text: Why is randomization important?

options.text: It reduces bias and allows causal conclusions (in experiments) or representative sampling

quiz_questions.explanation: Randomization is the foundation of valid inference.

quiz_questions.question_text: The normal condition for proportions is:
quiz_questions.explanation: Both np and n(1−p) ≥ 10 ensures the normal approximation is adequate.

quiz_questions.question_text: On the AP exam, 'interpret the confidence level' means:

options.text: The parameter is in the interval
If we repeated this process many times, about C% of the intervals would capture the true parameter
C% of the data is in the interval
We're C% sure the sample statistic is right

quiz_questions.explanation: Confidence level refers to the long-run capture rate of the procedure.

quiz_questions.question_text: A Type I error occurs when:

options.text: We fail to reject a false H₀
We reject H₀ when it is actually true (false positive)

quiz_questions.explanation: Type I error = incorrectly rejecting a true null hypothesis.

quiz_questions.question_text: A Type II error occurs when:

options.text: We fail to reject H₀ when it is actually false (false negative)

quiz_questions.explanation: Type II error = missing a real effect.

quiz_questions.question_text: Increasing sample size:

options.text: Decreases the margin of error and increases power

quiz_questions.explanation: Larger samples give more precise estimates and better power.

quiz_questions.question_text: On a free-response question about regression, you should:

options.text: Interpret slope and r² in context, check residual plot, and discuss conditions

quiz_questions.explanation: Complete regression answers include interpretation, conditions, and residual analysis.

quiz_questions.question_text: The purpose of a random sample is:

options.text: To reduce bias and allow generalization to the population

quiz_questions.explanation: Random sampling is the basis for extending conclusions from sample to population.

quiz_questions.question_text: Stratified sampling:

options.text: Divides the population into strata and samples from each, improving representation
Is the same as convenience sampling

quiz_questions.explanation: Stratification ensures all important subgroups are represented.

quiz_questions.question_text: The investigative task (Q6) on the AP exam typically:

options.text: Has multiple parts requiring you to integrate concepts and think critically

quiz_questions.explanation: Q6 tests deeper understanding across multiple topics.

quiz_questions.question_text: When there is a contradiction between the p-value and your expectation:

options.text: Report your findings honestly and discuss possible explanations
Redo the test until it works

quiz_questions.explanation: Statistical integrity requires honest reporting regardless of expectations.

quiz_questions.question_text: On multiple choice questions, eliminating wrong answers:

options.text: Can improve your score even when you're not sure of the right answer

quiz_questions.explanation: Process of elimination is a valuable strategy.

quiz_questions.question_text: For a two-sample t-test, degrees of freedom:

options.text: Are calculated using a formula (or use the smaller of n₁−1 and n₂−1 as a conservative estimate)

quiz_questions.explanation: df for two-sample t-tests use the Welch approximation or a conservative estimate.

quiz_questions.question_text: Writing 'We reject H₀' is incomplete unless you:

options.text: Also state what rejecting H₀ means in the context of the problem

quiz_questions.explanation: AP graders require contextual interpretation.

quiz_questions.question_text: The formula sheet is most useful when you:

options.text: Rely solely on intuition without checking the formula sheet
Know the concepts and use it as a reference for specific formulas during the exam

quiz_questions.explanation: Understanding when to use each formula is more important than pure memorization.

quiz_questions.question_text: A complete AP Statistics preparation includes:

options.text: Conceptual understanding, formula fluency, practice problems, and time management skills

quiz_questions.explanation: Well-rounded preparation covers all aspects.

quiz_questions.question_text: The most important takeaway from AP Statistics:

options.text: Statistical reasoning and data literacy are essential life skills for making informed decisions
Statistics is only for math class
Always trust the data without question

quiz_questions.explanation: AP Statistics teaches critical thinking about data that applies to every field and everyday life.
